TITLE: LA SCOLARITÉ OBLIGATOIRE ET SA PROLONGATION
paragraph: The Conference 1) Notes that the issue of compulsory schooling and its extension presents itself in very different ways across various countries and that no comprehensive measure can currently be recommended.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph highlights variability in compulsory schooling policies across countries and the need for monitoring, aligning with governance and planning. Accountability and transparency are relevant due to the emphasis on system monitoring. Other categories like access or curriculum are less directly supported by the text.
low_level_thinking.Legal frameworks: The paragraph discusses variability in compulsory schooling laws across countries and the lack of universal solutions, aligning with 'Education laws and regulations'. The mention of no comprehensive measure being recommended relates to 'Policy implementation and enforcement'.
low_level_thinking.Education system monitoring & evaluation: The paragraph discusses variability in compulsory schooling and the need for monitoring, aligning with education statistics and indicators. EMIS and data systems relate to monitoring systems. Institutional evaluation and inspection fit as the debate mentions monitoring and cross-cutting themes.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph discusses variability in compulsory schooling across countries and the lack of a universal solution, which aligns with cross-cutting themes. Other cross-cutting theme is the most appropriate category as it captures the general, non-specific nature of the discussion. Other categories like Citizenship and civic education or Sustainable development and climate action are too specific or unrelated to the main point about compulsory schooling variability.
low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph focuses on compulsory schooling variability and policy themes, aligning with general academic education orientation. Vocational/technical and combined categories are excluded as the text does not mention specific vocational aspects or combine general/vocational themes.

TITLE: LA SCOLARITÉ OBLIGATOIRE ET SA PROLONGATION
paragraph: 2) Recognizes that in countries where the number of schools does not yet correspond to the school-age population, the issue is not so much extending schooling as ensuring that every child has the opportunity to attend school for a minimum specified number of years.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes ensuring access to education for all children, aligning with 'Access and equity'. It also discusses policy frameworks and system monitoring, which relate to 'Governance and planning'. Other categories like 'Quality and learning outcomes' or 'Financing' are less directly relevant to the focus on access and policy structure.
low_level_thinking.Education system monitoring & evaluation: The paragraph discusses monitoring school access and ensuring minimum attendance, which relates to education statistics and indicators. EMIS and data systems are relevant for tracking school numbers and student enrollment. Other categories like learning assessments or quality assurance are less directly relevant to the focus on school availability and minimum attendance.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph focuses on ensuring equitable access to education and addressing under-resourced regions, aligning with inclusive education. 'Other cross-cutting theme' is retained as the core issue of equitable access is not explicitly covered by other categories.
low_level_thinking.Learner population: The paragraph focuses on ensuring all children attend school for a minimum number of years, directly aligning with 'Children (0-17)'. Other categories like 'All learners' or 'Adults' are not emphasized, and the context is specifically about school-age populations.
low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph focuses on ensuring equitable access to education and minimum years of schooling, which aligns with general academic education goals. Vocational/technical and combined categories are less relevant as the focus is on universal access rather than skill-specific training.

TITLE: LA SCOLARITÉ OBLIGATOIRE ET SA PROLONGATION
paragraph: 3) While acknowledging that the number of compulsory school years may vary between countries, considers it desirable that the number of actual years of schooling should in no case be less than seven, and notes that this minimum is already exceeded in many countries.

low_level_thinking.Education level: The paragraph focuses on compulsory schooling minimums, aligning with primary education (ISCED 1). Other categories like secondary or higher education are not directly relevant to the seven-year minimum discussed.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes a minimum seven-year schooling requirement, aligning with quality and equity goals. Governance is needed for policy consistency. Other categories like financing or curriculum are less directly relevant to the focus on minimum years and global enforcement.
low_level_thinking.Legal frameworks: The paragraph discusses a legal standard for compulsory schooling, aligning with education laws. It also mentions the need for global policy consistency and enforcement, which falls under policy implementation. Other categories like international conventions are less relevant here.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph focuses on policy consistency and legal enforcement for education quality, aligning with inclusive education. 'Other cross-cutting theme' is retained as the main focus isn't explicitly covered by other categories.
low_level_thinking.Education system monitoring & evaluation: The paragraph discusses minimum schooling requirements and data on compulsory education years, aligning with education statistics. It also implies the need for data systems (EMIS) to track and enforce these standards, while other categories like learning assessments or quality assurance are less directly relevant to the data and policy focus.

TITLE: LA SCOLARITÉ OBLIGATOIRE ET SA PROLONGATION
paragraph: 4) Considers that the school leaving age should not be set before the point at which the physical, intellectual, and moral education of the child has been sufficiently ensured.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes ensuring comprehensive child development before setting the school leaving age, aligning with 'Quality and learning outcomes' as it relates to educational requirements. 'Other policy theme' is selected due to the unique focus on child development timing not covered by other categories.
low_level_thinking.Legal frameworks: The paragraph discusses legal considerations for setting the school leaving age, aligning with 'Education laws and regulations' as it addresses legal frameworks for education policy. 'Legal disputes and interpretation' is relevant as the paragraph implies potential legal debates over the timing of school leaving age. Other categories like 'Constitution and fundamental rights' are less directly relevant to the specific legal framework discussed.
low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes comprehensive child development in physical, intellectual, and moral education, aligning with general academic education. Vocational/technical and combined categories are excluded as the focus is on holistic education rather than specific skill training or integration of academic and vocational elements.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes ensuring child development before setting the school leaving age, aligning with human rights to holistic development. Inclusive education is relevant as it addresses the needs of all children. Other categories like citizenship or health are less directly tied to the specific focus on development timing and equity.
low_level_thinking.Learner population: The paragraph focuses on child development before setting the school leaving age, directly aligning with 'Children (0-17)'. Other categories like 'Youth and adolescents' or 'Adults' are not relevant here, as the discussion centers on children's comprehensive education.

TITLE: LA SCOLARITÉ OBLIGATOIRE ET SA PROLONGATION
paragraph: 5) Draws the attention of Governments to the importance of ensuring that the adoption of the principle of compulsory schooling and sanctions for breaches of this principle are accompanied by the necessary effort on the part of the authorities to make full compliance with this obligation possible.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes government attention to policy implementation and monitoring, aligning with governance and planning. Accountability and transparency are relevant due to the focus on ensuring compliance through legal frameworks and system monitoring. Other categories like access or financing are not directly addressed.
low_level_thinking.Legal frameworks: The paragraph discusses government responsibility to ensure compliance with compulsory schooling, which relates to education laws and regulations. It also mentions the need for measures enabling compliance, which aligns with policy implementation and enforcement. Other categories like constitutional rights or international treaties are less directly relevant here.
low_level_thinking.Education system monitoring & evaluation: The paragraph emphasizes government attention to compliance mechanisms, which relates to monitoring systems (Education statistics and indicators), data systems (EMIS and data systems), and institutional oversight (Institutional evaluation and inspection). Other categories like learning assessments or research are less directly relevant to the monitoring and compliance focus.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes government responsibility and compliance mechanisms, aligning with citizenship and civic education. Inclusive education is relevant as it relates to ensuring all students can meet obligations. Other categories like human rights or sustainability are less directly tied to the specific focus on government policy and compliance.
low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph focuses on policy themes and legal frameworks related to compulsory schooling, which align with general academic education orientation. Vocational/technical and combined categories are excluded as the text does not mention specific technical training or combine general/vocational elements.

TITLE: LA SCOLARITÉ OBLIGATOIRE ET SA PROLONGATION
paragraph: 6) Considers that, in principle, and for the majority of countries, extending schooling beyond the age of 14 would offer indisputable advantages, except for temporary and short-duration exemptions that could be granted in certain cases, for example during agricultural work periods.

low_level_thinking.Education level: The paragraph discusses extending schooling beyond 14, aligning with secondary education (ISCED 2/3). Other categories like higher education or primary are not relevant to extending schooling beyond 14.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph discusses extending schooling beyond 14, which relates to curriculum and pedagogy. Temporary exemptions involve governance and planning. 'Other policy theme' is included as the main focus isn't explicitly covered by other categories.
low_level_thinking.Legal frameworks: The paragraph discusses extending schooling beyond 14 and temporary exemptions, which relates to education laws and regulations. Policy implementation and enforcement are relevant as it addresses how such policies are applied and managed. Other categories like international conventions or constitutional rights are less directly relevant to the specific policy and legal framework discussed.
low_level_thinking.Education system monitoring & evaluation: The paragraph discusses policy implications of extending schooling, which relates to education statistics and indicators. EMIS and data systems are relevant for tracking school enrollment and policy implementation. Other categories like learning assessments or quality assurance are less directly addressed.
low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph discusses extending schooling beyond 14 and temporary exemptions, which relates to implementing extended curricula and evaluating reforms. Other categories like 'Design and development' or 'Content and learning areas' are less directly relevant to the policy and reform focus of the debate.
low_level_thinking.Pedagogy & assessment: The paragraph discusses the theoretical basis for extending schooling and the implications for policy and assessment. 'Pedagogical foundations and theories' reflects the principle-based argument for extended schooling. 'Assessment and feedback' relates to the evaluation of educational outcomes. Other categories like 'Teaching methods' or 'Technology-supported pedagogy' are not directly addressed.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph discusses policy considerations and legal frameworks for extending schooling, aligning with 'Government and policy makers'. 'Other' is included due to the general stakeholder focus mentioned in the summary, while other categories like teachers, parents, etc., are not explicitly addressed.

TITLE: LA SCOLARITÉ OBLIGATOIRE ET SA PROLONGATION
paragraph: 7) Proclaims that the issue of extending schooling must be resolved in conjunction with that of the age of admission to work.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes integrating schooling extension with work admission, requiring coordinated governance. Accountability is needed for system monitoring. Other categories like access or curriculum are less directly relevant to the policy coordination theme.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph discusses policy integration between schooling and work admission, which aligns with cross-cutting themes but does not fit specific categories. Other cross-cutting theme best captures the general policy and systemic integration focus.
low_level_thinking.Education system monitoring & evaluation: The paragraph discusses policy integration and system monitoring, aligning with education statistics and indicators for data-driven policy. EMIS and data systems are relevant for monitoring educational systems. Institutional evaluation and inspection fit as the debate emphasizes system monitoring and policy integration.

TITLE: LA SCOLARITÉ OBLIGATOIRE ET SA PROLONGATION
paragraph: 8) Wishes that, at the national level, the most complete coordination exists, with regard to the measures to be taken, between the Departments of Public Education and those of Labor, and that, at the international level, studies concerning the minimum age for admission to employment be conducted concurrently with those concerning compulsory schooling.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes national coordination between education and labor departments, aligning with governance and planning. It also mentions international research, fitting international cooperation. Other categories like access, quality, or financing are not directly addressed.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes coordination between education and labor departments (cross-cutting themes & skills) and international research on employment age and schooling (other cross-cutting theme). Other categories like citizenship, human rights, or sustainability are not directly addressed.
low_level_thinking.Education system monitoring & evaluation: The paragraph mentions international studies on employment age and compulsory schooling, aligning with 'Research and surveys'. It also references coordination between departments, implying data collection and monitoring, which fits 'Education statistics and indicators'.
low_level_thinking.Legal frameworks: The paragraph emphasizes national coordination between education and labor departments, aligning with 'Policy implementation and enforcement'. International research on employment age and schooling relates to 'International conventions and treaties'.

TITLE: LA SCOLARITÉ OBLIGATOIRE ET SA PROLONGATION
paragraph: 9) Wishes that the education provided in the proper school setting be extended into a post-school education that is also compulsory; that the extended education, whether school-based or post-school, consist essentially of general training, drawing its interests and teaching content from the dominant concerns of the region, whether rural, industrial, commercial, etc., and, for young girls, include home economics preparation; and that, by giving greater emphasis to practical exercises, it aims to awaken and develop the students' vocational abilities; a significant place should be reserved for physical and moral training.

low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph emphasizes general training aligned with regional concerns and home economics, fitting 'General (academic)'. Vocational skills and practical exercises align with 'Vocational/technical (TVET)'. 'Combined' is excluded as the text does not explicitly blend both aspects.
low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph discusses curriculum design (Design and development), content alignment with regional needs (Content and learning areas), vocational and competency development (Competency-based and learning outcomes), and broader curriculum-related issues (Other). Excluded categories like Organization and instructional time and Curriculum implementation were not directly addressed.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes vocational skills, practical exercises, and physical/moral training, aligning with life skills. Health and well-being is included due to physical training. 'Other cross-cutting theme' covers remaining elements like citizenship and regional concerns not fully captured by other categories.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes curriculum design aligned with regional needs and vocational training, fitting 'Curriculum and pedagogy policies'. Extending education beyond school aligns with 'Lifelong learning and adult education'. Home economics for girls and physical/moral training relate to 'Inclusion and diversity'.
low_level_thinking.Education system monitoring & evaluation: The paragraph discusses policy frameworks and curriculum design for extended education, aligning with institutional evaluation. The emphasis on regional needs and vocational training suggests research-based policy development, fitting 'Research and surveys'.
low_level_thinking.Legal frameworks: The paragraph discusses extending compulsory education and curriculum design, which aligns with education laws and regulations. Policy implementation and enforcement are relevant as the text mentions policy frameworks and education orientation. Other categories like constitutional rights or international treaties are not directly addressed.

TITLE: LA SCOLARITÉ OBLIGATOIRE ET SA PROLONGATION
paragraph: 10) Considers it desirable that any measure concerning the extension of schooling be taken with due regard to the necessity of coordinating the various types of education and ensuring the continuity of their curricula.

low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph focuses on coordinating education types and ensuring curriculum continuity, which aligns with 'Content and learning areas' for curriculum structure and 'Curriculum implementation' for ensuring continuity. Other categories like 'Design and development' or 'Organization and instructional time' are less directly relevant to the specific coordination and continuity emphasis.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes curriculum continuity and coordination of education types, directly aligning with 'Curriculum and pedagogy policies'. Coordination and planning are central to 'Governance and planning'. Other categories like 'Access and equity' or 'Financing' are not directly addressed.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes coordination and curriculum continuity, which align with inclusive education principles. 'Other cross-cutting theme' is retained as coordination and continuity are not explicitly covered by other categories. Other categories like 'Life skills' or 'Sustainable development' are not directly relevant here.
low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph focuses on coordinating education types and ensuring curriculum continuity, which aligns with general academic education. Vocational/technical and combined categories are excluded as the text does not mention specific technical training or integration of vocational and general education.
